button.subtitle: A basic pressable element that triggers an action. Renders as a native button on each platform.
button.description:
A basic pressable element that triggers an action. Renders as a native button on each platform — a `UIButton` on iOS and an `android.widget.Button` on Android — so its appearance follows the OS styling by default.

Because `Button` delegates rendering to the platform, its customisation surface is intentionally minimal. For full visual control use `Pressable` or `TouchableOpacity` instead.

The `onPress` handler receives a `GestureResponderEvent` but it is rarely needed; most handlers ignore the argument entirely.

props.title: Text label rendered inside the button. On Android the OS automatically uppercases it.
props.onPress: Fired when the button is pressed.
props.color: Text color on iOS, background color on Android.
props.disabled: Disables interaction and renders in a muted style.

gotchas.gotcha1: *iOS vs Android* — `color` sets the text color on iOS but the background fill on Android. A single value will look inconsistent across platforms.
gotchas.gotcha2: *No style prop* — `Button` doesn't accept `style`. Any customisation beyond `color` and `disabled` requires `Pressable` or `TouchableOpacity`.
gotchas.gotcha3: *Android* — The `title` is automatically uppercased by the OS. Use `Pressable` with a `Text` child if case matters.
gotchas.gotcha4: *Touch target* — React Native recommends 44×44pt minimum. `Button` doesn't enforce this — small labels produce small buttons.
